# Prior implementations the `provenance` gate must refuse to let through.
#
# One commit per line. Anything reachable here is compared, byte for byte, against
# every PR that touches product code (see scripts/ci/verify-fresh-authoring.sh).
#
# You do not need to list reverted work — the gate auto-discovers the parents of
# any `Revert "..."` commit on main, and it always compares against the tip of
# main itself. This file is for implementations that are NOT reachable that way,
# and for pinning the ones we care most about so the check is legible on the PR.
#
# NEVER remove an entry to make a red check go green. If you believe the gate has
# a false positive, say so on the PR and let a human decide. The whole point of
# this file is that it is harder to quietly delete a line than to quietly copy a
# file.

# ── The first bead: celestial unit-vector conversion + angular distance ───────
# Four attempts, three of which produced code. The task is tightly specified, so
# a genuinely fresh implementation still differs by hundreds of lines (run 3 vs
# run 1: 356). A restore differs by zero.

# Run 1 (ps-5q6, PR #18). The original implementation — free functions.
21c71d4e636d65ecaacfc2351d7a9e7996e0b3b4

# Run 2b (ps-66m, PR #22). NOT a second implementation: a byte-identical restore
# of run 1, recovered from a stale polecat branch and produced in 90 seconds. Ten
# green checks and an APPROVE from the judge. This commit is why the gate exists;
# it is the gate's regression test.
66a88b7c5d4afb9c82cefe945595f09264cc371d

# Run 3 (ps-math-01, PR #24). Authored from the spec — a UnitVector struct rather
# than free functions, 86.57% coverage. This is what a clean run looks like.
7a428c7c01ba1f1cf9d821e9e4e81db4af464ce0
